intro: Romulea caulescens is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Iridaceae, commonly known as the iris family. This plant has been traditionally used in African medicine, and its roots have been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Romulea caulescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
